summary: Iteration 106: Tested daily grouped views for Sales and Expenses pages with pagination. All 14 backend tests passed. Sales shows daily totals with Date/Total/Cash/Bank/Online/Credit/Branches columns. Expenses shows expandable daily rows - clicking expands to show individual entries with Category/Description/Branch/Amount/Payment Mode/Delete. Pagination controls are present in code but hidden because current dataset (52 sales, 51 expenses) is below the 200-record threshold.
critical_code_review_comments: Minor hydration warnings in VirtualizedTable due to span elements inside table structure - not blocking but should be addressed in future cleanup
context_for_next_testing_agent: Daily grouped views implemented: Sales shows one row per date with payment mode breakdown (Cash/Bank/Online/Credit) and branch totals - no expand needed. Expenses shows one row per date with expand functionality - clicking ChevronRight icon expands to show individual entries with Category/Description/Branch/Amount/Payment Mode/Delete button. Pagination controls (Previous/Next) appear at bottom when totalPages > 1 (i.e., when records exceed 200 per page limit). Current dataset has 52 sales and 51 expenses so pagination is correctly hidden.

1_sales_daily_grouped_view.details: Sales table shows Date, Total, Cash, Bank, Online, Credit, Branches columns. Each row is one date showing aggregated totals with entry count. Uses VirtualizedTable for performance.

2_sales_pagination.details: Backend API returns {data, total, page, limit, pages}. Frontend has Previous/Next buttons at data-testid='sales-prev-page' and 'sales-next-page'. Controls only visible when totalPages > 1 (>200 records with default limit). Currently hidden because only 52 sales records exist.

3_expenses_daily_grouped_view.details: Expenses table shows Date, Total, Cash, Bank, Credit, Categories columns. Each row is one date with entry count. Shows 'Showing X expenses across Y days' footer.

4_expenses_expandable_rows.details: Clicking day row expands to show individual expenses. ChevronRight becomes ChevronDown on expansion. Expanded entries show Category badge, Description, Branch, Amount, Payment Mode badge, and Delete button. Uses expandedDates state to track which dates are expanded.

5_expenses_pagination.details: Backend API returns paginated structure. Frontend has Previous/Next buttons at data-testid='expenses-prev-page' and 'expenses-next-page'. Controls only visible when totalPages > 1. Currently hidden because only 51 expense records exist.

6_backend_pagination_api.details: Both /api/sales and /api/expenses accept page and limit query params. Return {data, total, page, limit, pages} structure. Correctly calculates pages = ceil(total/limit). Handles edge cases like page beyond total.
